title: When have I rushed or waited too long
subtitle: A journal set for mapping your relationship with timing around money decisions.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 48 of the MONEY program. The prompts trace specific times when timing fear shaped a financial decision — and what you would do differently now.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 48 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you're in a timing decision — waiting to act, or about to act, and uncertainty about the moment is making it harder.

content.text: A journal set from Day 48 of the MONEY program. The prompts look backward at specific timing decisions — to build an evidence base for trusting yourself, rather than defaulting to pattern.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
How do you currently feel about your sense of timing around money and opportunity?
Write a quick paragraph. No editing.
Think of a time you moved on an opportunity and it worked out. What gave you the signal to go?
Write the specifics. What were the conditions? What did you know?
Think of a time you rushed into a money decision out of fear of missing out. What happened?
Write what was driving the rush. What were you afraid of losing?
Think of a time you waited too long and felt you'd missed the window. What did the hesitation cost?
Write what held you back. The fear underneath the hesitation.
What would trusting your timing look like, practically? What would you do more of? Less of?
Write it as a concrete behaviour, not an attitude.
Adapted from Day 48 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
